stem: [Licensing & Regulation (CA)] Which activity most clearly requires an active life agent license in California?
choices: Providing technical support for an insurer’s website
Soliciting and negotiating a life insurance policy for compensation
Explaining general financial concepts with no insurance discussion
Submitting a public records request to CDI
answer: Soliciting and negotiating a life insurance policy for compensation
rationale: Licensing is required when soliciting/negotiating or otherwise transacting insurance for compensation.

stem: [Licensing & Regulation (CA)] A life agent moves to a new address. What is the safest compliance-oriented action?
choices: Use the old address on applications to avoid delays
Only tell the insurer you represent; no need to update CDI
Update the licensing record promptly per CDI requirements and keep proof of submission
Wait until license renewal to update
answer: Update the licensing record promptly per CDI requirements and keep proof of submission
rationale: Keeping CDI records current supports compliance and avoids administrative issues.

stem: [Licensing & Regulation (CA)] After multiple failed attempts, what happens if an examinee fails the same CDI license exam 10 times?
choices: They can only test online going forward
They are barred from taking that exam for 12 months from the last failed attempt
They must wait 30 days
They can retest immediately with a higher fee
answer: They are barred from taking that exam for 12 months from the last failed attempt
rationale: CDI applies a statutory retake limit; after 10 failures, a 12‑month bar applies.

stem: [Licensing & Regulation (CA)] For CDI attempt limits, how are combination exams treated when they include the life license type?
choices: Attempts are pooled across all license types
They count toward the life exam attempt limit
Only the first attempt counts
They never count toward limits
answer: They count toward the life exam attempt limit
rationale: Combo exams that include life are considered the same exam type for the life exam limit.

stem: [Licensing & Regulation (CA)] Why should your legal name match your government ID when registering and fingerprinting?
choices: It guarantees same-day licensure
It increases your exam score
A mismatch can delay eligibility, check-in, and licensing processing
It reduces CE requirements
answer: A mismatch can delay eligibility, check-in, and licensing processing
rationale: Identity mismatches commonly cause processing delays at testing and fingerprinting.

stem: [Licensing & Regulation (CA)] Which statements are generally true? (Select all that apply.)
choices: All policies have identical provisions
Underwriting helps manage adverse selection
Policy provisions define rights and duties of parties
Premiums are refunded automatically if no claim occurs
Insurance eliminates all loss possibility
Insurance transfers risk from the individual to the insurer
answer: Insurance transfers risk from the individual to the insurer
Underwriting helps manage adverse selection
Policy provisions define rights and duties of parties
rationale: Insurance spreads risk; underwriting classifies risk; policy provisions define contract obligations.

stem: [Ethics & Insurance Fraud] The most ethically correct approach when recommending life insurance is to:
choices: Recommend the product with the highest commission
Avoid documenting the recommendation to reduce liability
Guarantee investment returns to reassure the client
Place the client’s interest first and recommend coverage aligned with documented needs
answer: Place the client’s interest first and recommend coverage aligned with documented needs
rationale: Ethical practice prioritizes the client’s interests, suitability, and honest communication.

stem: [Ethics & Insurance Fraud] Which action is most likely to be considered insurance fraud?
choices: Explaining policy exclusions carefully
Scheduling a needs-analysis review meeting
Falsifying material information on an application to obtain coverage or a lower premium
Delivering a policy after first premium is paid
answer: Falsifying material information on an application to obtain coverage or a lower premium
rationale: Material misrepresentation on applications is a common fraud scenario.

stem: [Ethics & Insurance Fraud] A client asks you to 'leave the medical section blank.' Best response?
choices: Tell them blanks are acceptable if premium is prepaid
Suggest guessing to speed up the process
Decline and explain that complete, truthful answers are required; recommend the client answer accurately
Agree; underwriting will figure it out
answer: Decline and explain that complete, truthful answers are required; recommend the client answer accurately
rationale: Agents must avoid assisting misrepresentation and ensure accurate applications.

stem: [Ethics & Insurance Fraud] Best documentation practice to reduce ethical and E&O risk is:
choices: Rely only on verbal explanations
Document only if the client complains
Discard notes after policy delivery
Record the needs analysis, disclosures, and the client’s decisions in clear, dated notes
answer: Record the needs analysis, disclosures, and the client’s decisions in clear, dated notes
rationale: Clear documentation supports transparency, compliance, and defensibility.

stem: [Insurance Concepts & Terms] Which statement best describes the purpose of risk pooling?
choices: It eliminates all risk for insurers
It spreads losses of the few across many policyholders through premiums
It guarantees profit for the insured
It replaces underwriting entirely
answer: It spreads losses of the few across many policyholders through premiums
rationale: Pooling and the law of large numbers help insurers predict losses and price coverage.

Policy provisions define rights and duties of parties
Insurance transfers risk from the individual to the insurer

Underwriting helps manage adverse selection
Policy provisions define rights and duties of parties
rationale: Insurance spreads risk; underwriting classifies risk; policy provisions define contract obligations.

Promise the new policy is always better

rationale: Replacement rules focus on disclosure, documentation, and avoiding misleading comparisons.

stem: [Taxation Basics (Life & Annuities)] Which statement about life insurance death benefits is most accurate in general?
choices: They are always subject to payroll tax
They are always taxable as ordinary income
They are never taxable under any circumstance
They are generally received income-tax free by the beneficiary (with exceptions)
answer: They are generally received income-tax free by the beneficiary (with exceptions)
rationale: Death benefits are generally income-tax free, though exceptions exist (e.g., interest, certain transfers).
